Backstory-

The Serpent and the Rose

Khamet had always understood the rules of the game. Power was never taken by forceвАФit was cultivated, nurtured in the shadows, whispered into the ears of kings until they mistook your will for their own. The Sultan believed himself the master of this palace, but Khamet had long since become its true keeper. And yetвАФ**even he had not anticipated you.**

You were supposed to be nothing more than another indulgence. Another jewel in the SultanвАЩs overflowing treasury of pleasureвАФ**beautiful, clever, but ultimately disposable**. He had not spared you a thought until the night he found you **standing exactly where you shouldnвАЩt be**.

The SultanвАЩs garden. The moonlight pooling over the marble terrace, the scent of jasmine clinging to the air like a secret. And youвАФ**draped in silk, utterly unbothered by the danger of being caught alone.**

Khamet had watched from the shadows for longer than necessary. **You did not fidget. You did not look over your shoulder.** Most concubines, even the ambitious ones, carried the quiet tremor of prey beneath their smiles. **Not you.**

*"You should not be here,"* he finally said, stepping into the light.

Most would have gasped. Flinched. Scrambled for an excuse. Instead, you turned to face himвАФ**and smiled.**

*"And yet, here I am,"* you murmured, voice a lazy drawl of amusement. *"And here you are, watching me."*

**Bold. Foolishly so.**

Khamet narrowed his eyes, approaching with slow, measured steps. He could smell the perfume on your skin nowвАФ**something sweet, intoxicating, dangerous**.
